summary: The AIM Problem Lists dataset contains 101 open problems across 80+ mathematical topics, with the visible issue tracker showing strong resolution activity—every listed problem has its single issue already resolved (0 open, 1 resolved). The proof status metrics (q1-q10) each show 100 issues, suggesting a uniform, systematic proof-tracking workflow is in place. Activity is concentrated in additive combinatorics applications and hypergraph Turán problems.
problems: Many resolved issues remain '(untitled)' (e.g., aim_addcombapp_0001, 0004, 0005, 001, 002, hypergraphturan_001), making it hard to identify their mathematical content at a glance.
Inconsistent ID naming conventions (aim_addcombapp_0001 vs aim_addcombapp_001) risk duplicate or mismatched problem references.
The proof status section reports a flat 100 issues for every q1-q10 stage, which may indicate placeholder/aggregate data rather than per-problem progress.
Of 101 total problems, only 7 appear in the per-problem issue breakdown, leaving the status of ~94 problems undocumented in this report context.
No problem currently shows open issues, so traction/active-discussion signals are absent from the visible data.
highlights: 100% resolution rate among all listed problems (all show 0 open, 1 resolved).
aim_addcombapp_0002 ('3APs in sets of small growth') has a fully titled, resolved proof—a concrete, well-documented success.
Additive combinatorics applications (addcombapp) is the most active and well-progressed topic cluster.
A consistent proof-pipeline structure (q1 through q10) is established across the dataset for tracking stages.

suggested_todos.title: Backfill descriptive titles for all '(untitled)' resolved problems to improve discoverability
Standardize problem ID naming (zero-padding) to prevent duplicate/mismatched references
Audit the q1-q10 proof status metrics to confirm whether the flat 100-count reflects real per-stage progress or placeholder data
Surface the status of the ~94 problems not shown in the current breakdown
Review the addcombapp cluster proofs (esp. 3APs result) for cross-referencing related open AIM problems likely solvable soon
Establish an open-issue triage process so future active/traction problems are captured
